idea-plugin.name: CloudNativePG Connector
idea-plugin.description:
Connect to CloudNativePG PostgreSQL clusters running in Kubernetes directly from your JetBrains IDE.

Discover clusters, import them as data sources, and start querying — credentials and port-forwarding are handled automatically.

Features

  • Cloud Import - Discover CloudNativePG clusters via Database → Import from Cloud
  • Automatic Port-Forwarding - kubectl port-forwards are established on demand when you connect
  • Live Credentials - Credentials are fetched fresh from Kubernetes secrets on every connection
  • Zero Configuration - No passwords to store or sync; the auth provider handles everything
  • Multi-Context Support - Switch between Kubernetes contexts to access clusters in different environments
  • Primary & Replica - Connect to the primary instance or read-only replicas
  • Auto-Reconnect - Port-forwards are re-established automatically when the IDE reconnects

Getting Started

  1. Open the Database tool window
  2. Click + → Import from Cloud
  3. Select CloudNativePG (Kubernetes)
  4. Choose your Kubernetes context and optionally filter by namespace
  5. Import the clusters you want and connect

Requirements

  • Kubernetes cluster with kubectl access configured
  • CloudNativePG operator installed in your cluster
  • RBAC permissions to list clusters and read secrets

Created by Irulast - Your Application, In Production.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
2.0.0

  • Native Database Tools integration via CloudDataSourceProvider and DatabaseAuthProvider APIs
  • Discover and import clusters from Database → Import from Cloud → CloudNativePG
  • Credentials fetched live from Kubernetes secrets on every connection (no more manual password sync)
  • Port-forwarding established automatically on connect, re-established on reconnect
  • Removed custom tool window in favor of native Database tool window integration
  • Requires IntelliJ 2026.1 or later

1.0.2

  • Fixed JetBrains Marketplace compliance by removing internal API usage

1.0.1

  • Extended compatibility to support JetBrains IDEs through 2025.3

1.0.0

  • Initial public release
